segments.s580-g1: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. شاپور is the personal name (here the Sasanian king). بنشست is a past‑tense verb 'sat down / took his seat'. بر + جای is the preposition + noun 'on/at the place (seat)'; the whole phrase means 'when Shapur sat on the seat'. عم is an old/poetic noun placed after جای specifying whose place or what sort of place; here it functions as a modifier of جای.
segments.s580-g2: از ایران functions as a prepositional phrase 'regarding / for Iran' (از here = concerning). بسی is an emphatic/adverbial classical word meaning 'very/much'. شاد is a predicate adjective 'glad/happy'. و links the two adjectival/predicative phrases. بهری is a prepositional/relational word 'with regard to / in respect of' and دژم is an adjective 'grim / stern', so the clause gives two qualities: very glad for Iran and stern in purpose.
segments.s580-g3: چنین گفت is a formula 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus', گفت = 'said'). کای is a vocative particle 'O' addressing someone; نامور modifies it ('O renowned one'). بخردان is a dative/benefactive form meaning 'to the wise' (بخردان = 'to the wise / prudent ones'), so this segment is an address to distinguished listeners.
segments.s580-g4: This segment lists the addressees or descriptors: جهاندیده is an adjective 'worldly‑wise' or 'experienced in the world', و = 'and', رای‌زن is a compound participial adjective 'one who utters/dispenses counsel' (literally 'counsel‑striking' → 'counselor'), and موبدان (mōbedān) is the plural 'mobeds' (Zoroastrian priests). The sequence is a coordinative apposition describing the intended audience.
segments.s580-g5: بدانید is an imperative 'know' or 'be aware'. کان here is an archaic demonstrative/introducer used with کس to mean 'that/any such person' (classical 'whoever' flavor). کس که گوید دروغ is a relative clause 'whoever says a lie' (که = 'who/that', گوید = present/subjunctive 'says/utter', دروغ = 'lie').
segments.s580-g6: نگیرد is a negative subjunctive/future‑oriented verb 'shall not take/receive' (negated form of گرفتن). ازین‌پس is a temporal phrase 'from now on / henceforth' composed of از ('from') + ین (classical spelling of این 'this') + پس ('after'). بر ما فروغ literally 'radiance upon us' = 'favor/glory from us' (فروغ = radiance/splendor). The clause therefore states that such a person will no longer receive our radiance/honor.
segments.s580-g7: دروغ از بر ما نباشد is a negative optative/jussive construction meaning 'let there be no lie among us' (دروغ = 'falsehood', از بر ما = 'from/upon us, among us', نباشد = negative subjunctive/jussive 'may it not be / let it not be'). ز رای here begins the reason phrase 'on account of / in matters of counsel'.
segments.s580-g8: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for / because'. از رای باشد بزرگی به جای means 'greatness is from (or comes by) counsel and is in its place'—از رای ('from counsel'), باشد is the verb 'is/comes to be' (subjunctive/third‑person), بزرگی = 'greatness', به جای = 'in its proper place/position'. The clause gives the rationale: greatness depends on right counsel and occupies its rightful place.
segments.s580-g25: اگر is the conditional particle 'if'. سفله‌گر is a compound noun meaning a base or lowly person; here it functions as the subject of the implied clause. مرد follows in apposition or as a predicate noun ('become/turn into a man' — the copula is omitted as is common in poetry). با + [شرم و راد] is an instrumental/qualifying phrase 'with modesty and generosity', where راد is an adjective/noun meaning 'magnanimous/generous'.
segments.s580-g26: به آزادگی is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in/with freedom' or 'freely' (به here gives an adverbial/goal-like sense). یک دل و یک نهاد is a parallel noun phrase 'one heart and one nature/disposition' (نهاد = innate disposition); the two nouns are coordinated to describe a single unified inner state.
segments.s580-g27: میانه گزیند is a verb phrase 'chooses moderation/the middle course' where گزیند is the classical present 3rd-person form of گزینیدن ('chooses'). ز کار 'in/from his deeds' (ز = from/about/of) specifies the domain of choice. The segment begins with a relative/particle (کو in the printed line) introducing 'he who...', used here as 'he who/one who'.
segments.s580-g28: آیدش is a verb 'comes to him' (آید + enclitic ش = 'to him/for him'); بسند is from بسیدن/بس (to suffice) and here works as 'may suffice' / 'is sufficient'. بخشش کردگار is the noun phrase 'the forgiveness (or grant) of the Creator/God' and is the thing that 'comes to him' and 'suffices'. Word order is verb–object–dative enclitic typical of classical Persian poetry.
segments.s580-g29: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. بپراگند is a verbal form meaning 'scatters/spreads' (classical present); بر گزاف is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'upon/for vain reasons' or 'needlessly/groundlessly' (گزاف = groundless/excessive). The verb and the adverbial phrase describe the behavior of the 'fourth' type.
segments.s580-g30: همی is an adverb meaning 'constantly/always' (an emphatic classical particle). دانشی here functions partitive/indefinite 'some knowledge' or 'a bit of learning'; نام جوید 'seeks a name/reputation' (نام = fame, جوید = seeks). ز لاف 'from/by boasting' (ز = from/because of; لاف = boast). Together: 'constantly, (with his) little knowledge he seeks fame through boasting.'
segments.s580-g31: دو گیتی (literally 'the two worlds') is the object placed before the verb for emphasis ('both worlds'). بیابد is the classical present tense 'finds/attains'. دل مرد راد is a genitival phrase 'the heart of a generous man' (ezāfe is omitted in poetic diction). The sentence literally: 'The heart of a generous man attains both worlds.'
segments.s580-g32: نباشد is negative of بودن in classical order and here means 'is not / will not be' (the negative predicative verb). دل سفله 'the heart of the base/lowly person' is the subject phrase; یک روز شاد is a temporal/adverbial phrase 'one day (even) happy' — placed after the verb for emphasis: 'is not happy even for one day.'
segments.s580-g49: ستاره is the subject ('star, stars') and زدند is a 3rd‑person plural past verb. In classical/poetic Persian زدَن is often used with celestial bodies to mean 'shine/appear' or 'rise', so here 'the stars rose/appeared.' از پی خوابگاه is a prepositional phrase ('from/away after the sleeping‑quarters') with پی meaning 'after/toward' and خوابگاه 'sleeping‑quarters/camp'; the phrase gives the source/direction.
segments.s580-g50: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. چیزی is an unspecified subject 'something'. بخورد here is a poetic/past usage of خورد meaning 'befall/happen' (not literal 'eat'). و links the clauses. بیاسود is an adjectival predicate 'uneasy/not at rest' that combines with شاه ('the king')—the copula is implied, so the adjective precedes the noun in a poetic inverted order: 'the king was uneasy.'
segments.s580-g51: سه جام is a numeral phrase 'three cups' (object). می‌خسروانی is an attributive compound 'kingly/royal (wine)' modifying جام. بخورد is a 3rd‑person past 'he drank' (here literal 'drink'), so the clause means 'he drank three cups of royal wine.' Word order: object phrase before the verb, as normal in Persian.
segments.s580-g52: پراندیشه 'pensive/thoughtful' + شد 'became' forms 'he became thoughtful.' Then سر سوی خواب کرد is a small clause: سر 'head' + سوی 'toward' + خواب 'sleep' with کرد ('did/turned') as the verb meaning 'he turned his head toward sleep.' The verb کرد completes the action; this is a sequence of two states/actions.
segments.s580-g53: پراگنده 'scattered' + گشتند (3rd‑person plural past of گشتن) = 'they became scattered' or 'were scattered.' لشکر همه ('the whole army') is the subject of that verb; plural verb marks the collective subject. Note the adjective+verb predicate precedes the subject in this ordering, which is common in epic diction.
segments.s580-g54: چو is 'when'. در خواب شد means 'became in sleep' — i.e., 'fell asleep' (شد as the copular/auxiliary). شهریار رمه places 'شهریار' (the king) and 'رمه' (his host/flock) together in apposition/possession: poetically 'the king's throng/host.' So the clause reads 'when the king's host fell asleep.'
segments.s580-g55: بخفت is the past verb 'he slept.' او is the free pronoun 'he' appearing after the verb (verb–subject order). و links to the next clause. از دشت برخاست باد is an inverted order: برخاست (rose/arose) + باد (wind) + از دشت (from the plain) — literally 'rose, a wind from the plain,' with باد as the subject that follows the verb here.
segments.s580-g56: که introduces a result/wish clause 'so that / such that.' کس is 'no one/anyone' (here in negative sense). باد in this position is the optative particle 'باد' meaning 'may' (an old/poetic wish marker), not the noun 'wind.' ازان سان 'from that sort' = 'such/that way.' ندارد is present‑tense 'has not' (negation) and with به یاد ('in memory') yields the meaning 'may no one have such a thing in memory' — a jussive/wishful negation expressed with باد + ندارد + به یاد.
segments.s580-g65: که‌گر (classical compound of که + گر) introduces a conditional clause ‘if’. بازیابی here functions as a finite verb idea ‘you find/obtain’ (the object is omitted and picked up from context). به + پیچی forms an adverbial phrase ‘by a trick/turn’ (به = by/with; پیچی = a turn/stratagem, used figuratively as ‘a cunning device’). بدرد is a verblike predicate ‘is of use / proves useful’ and links the conditional to the following prohibition.
segments.s580-g66: پژوهش مکن is a negative imperative: پژوهش (verbal noun ‘search/investigation’) + مکن (‘do not’), i.e. ‘do not investigate/seek’. گرد رازش مگرد is a second prohibitive clause: گرد here is verbal ‘go about/circle (round)’ and رازش is ‘its secret’ (راز + enclitic -ش), while مگرد is an archaic negative imperative form, so the phrase means ‘do not go circling about its secret / do not pry into its mystery’. The two prohibitions form a coordinated admonition.
segments.s580-g67: چنین است is a copular fronting meaning ‘thus/so it is’. کردار is the noun ‘deed/action/behaviour’ and is the semantic subject of the clause. این is the demonstrative ‘this’ modifying چرخ; چرخ تیر is a poetic compound — چرخ (‘wheel’, here meaning the wheel of fate/sky) qualified by تیر (‘arrow’), yielding the sense ‘this wheel of fate’ or ‘this shaft/bolt of the wheel’ describing the agent of that behaviour.
segments.s580-g68: The correlative construction چه ... چه ... means ‘whether ... or ...’. با is the preposition ‘with/toward’; مرد برنا = ‘a young man’ (مرد = man, برنا = youthful); مردپیر = ‘an old man’ (compound written together in poetry). The whole clause says the wheel’s action is the same whether applied to the young or to the old.
segments.s580-g17: اگر is the conditional marker 'if' at the start. دانشی مرد is a noun phrase 'a man of learning' (modifier before noun, literary order). راند is the past form of رانیدن used here poetically to mean 'sets (his) speech in motion / speaks', with سخن as its object following the verb.
segments.s580-g18: تو بشنو is an imperative: 'you, listen'. که introduces a result/purpose clause ('so that / in order that'). نگردد is negative subjunctive/present (ن- + گردد) meaning 'does not become' and کهن is the predicate 'old/ancient' — together 'that knowledge not become old.'
segments.s580-g19: دل مرد = 'the heart of the man' (possessive/genitive sense implied). مطمع is an epithet meaning one who expects/desires (here 'covetous' or 'expectant'). بود is the past copula 'was', and پر ز درد is a fixed phrase 'full of pain/sorrow' (پر + ز 'full of').
segments.s580-g20: به گرد طمع literally 'toward/around greed' (به گرد = around/toward), تا توانی = 'as long as you can' (تا + توانیدن, توانى = 'you can'). مگرد is a negative imperative 'do not turn / do not go' (here: 'do not turn toward greed'); the verb continues the prohibitive sense from the previous clause.
segments.s580-g21: مکن is the prohibitive imperative 'do not'. دوستی is the object 'friendship' (make/do not make friendship). با introduces the associate/instrument 'with', and دروغ آزمای functions as a descriptive phrase 'one who tries/tests by falsehood' (دروغ modifying آزمای), i.e. 'a person who tests using lies.'
segments.s580-g22: همان نیز = 'likewise / the same also' and here the verb is omitted and understood from the previous imperative (do not). با مرد ناپاک‌رای = 'with a man of unclean / base mind' (با introduces the association and ناپاک‌رای is an adjectival epithet).
segments.s580-g23: سرشت تن = 'the nature/constitution of the body' (noun + possessive relation). از چار گوهر = 'of four essences' (از + number + noun), and بود is the past copula 'was' — a simple descriptive clause about bodily composition.
segments.s580-g24: گذر here is the noun 'passage / escape'. زین چهارانش is a compressed classical phrase meaning 'from these four' (ز + این = 'from these' + چهارانش = 'their four' / 'these four'); کمتر بود = 'was less / was rarer' (comparative predicate). The list of words includes some rare or contracted forms (زین = ز این), so sense choices for those items are not directly given in the provided senses.
segments.s580-g33: بدین گیتی = a classical adverbial phrase 'in this world/this life' formed from the prepositional prefix ب- + a demonstrative/particle; it sits at the front to set the scene. او را بود نام زشت uses the past copula بود ('was/had') with the construction او را بود + نام: the clitic را emphasizes the following nominal 'name/reputation' as possessed by او (he) — literally 'to him there was a name ugly', i.e. 'he had an ugly reputation.'
segments.s580-g34: بدان گیتی‌اندر is another locative phrase ('in/unto that world' or 'in that realm'); the first token is a prepositional + demonstrative unit and the second explicitly marks 'in the world'. نیابد is the negative form of یافتن (here third person, classical/poetic present/subjunctive sense often translated as 'does not/will not attain/find'). بهشت is the object 'paradise.' The clause therefore states impossibility of attaining heaven in that world.
segments.s580-g35: دو گیتی here means 'the two worlds' (i.e. both this life and the next). نیابد is the negative 3rd‑person form of یافتن used in a general statement ('does not/does not attain'). دل مرد لاف = 'the heart (دل) of a man (مرد) of boast (لاف)' — the noun cluster means 'a boastful man' or 'the heart of the braggart.' Word order is topical (object/phrase fronting is common in epic Persian).
segments.s580-g36: که introduces the reason ('for/because'). بپراگند is a classical/poetic verbal form with the prefix ب- and a jussive/indicative sense 'he scatters' or 'he spreads.' خواسته is a verbal adjective / noun 'that which is wished' = 'desire(s)'; بر is the preposition 'on/upon'; گزاف is an adjective 'groundless/vain/absurd.' Together: 'because he scatters his desires upon the groundless.'
segments.s580-g37: ستوده کسی = 'a praiseworthy person' (ستوده as adjective). کو here functions as a relative/particle 'who/which' in classical usage (introducing the relative clause). میانه گزید is past tense گزید 'chose' with the noun میانه 'the middle/moderation' as object: 'who chose moderation.' The clause is a compact relative clause praising moderation.
segments.s580-g38: تن خویش را = 'his own self/body' (تن = body/self, خویش = 'his own', with the enclitic ش split here), where را marks the definite object. آفرین گسترید combines the noun/nominal آفرین 'praise/acclamation' with گسترید (past of گستردن) 'spread/extended' — literally 'he spread praise of his own self', i.e. he praised himself or extolled his person.
segments.s580-g39: شما را is topical/object marking 'for you' or 'to you' (را here emphasises the addressee). جهان‌آفرین is a compound 'world‑creator' (God/the Creator). یار is 'companion/ally' and باد is the optative particle 'may it be' expressing a wish. The whole is an optative benediction: 'May the world's Creator be your companion.'
segments.s580-g40: همیشه = 'always.' سر بخت = a bound phrase 'head of fortune' (سر = head/chief, بخت = fortune/luck); this idiom means one's good fortune or its leading aspect. بیدار = 'awake/alert' (adjective). باد again is the optative 'may it be', so the line is a wish: 'May the head of fortune always be alert.'
segments.s580-g9: Word order is archaic/poetic: همان ('that/the same') + مر (an old form of 'me'/'to me') precedes the noun phrase تن سفله را ('the lowly person/body' marked by را as definite object). دوستدار is a nominal/adjectival head meaning 'admirer/lover' (here describing that low-born person as someone who loves me). In effect the phrase says 'that same low-born person as a lover of mine'.
segments.s580-g10: نیابی is the negative present/subjunctive form of یافتن ('to find') — here 'you will not find' (impersonal/general you). به باغ اندرون gives the location 'in/into the inner garden/inner apartments' (به + place). چون نگار is a simile 'like the beloved' (چون = 'like', نگار = 'beloved').
segments.s580-g11: سری ('head') is the noun/topic, followed by را as a definite/marked object/topic. کجا here functions rhetorically as 'where/would' ('where would there be'), مغز ('brain') is the thing sought, باشد is the subjunctive/existential/old present of 'to be' ('there be'/'is'), and بسی ('enough') closes the clause — literally 'where in the head is (there) enough brain?'.
segments.s580-g12: This is an impersonal prohibition: گواژه ('testimony/evidence') is the topic, نباید ('one must not' / 'should not') is the modal negative, followed by the infinitive/verb زدن ('to give/record/utter' — here 'to give testimony'), and بر کسی ('against anyone') indicates the target of such testimony. Together: 'one should not give testimony against anyone.'
segments.s580-g13: زبان را نگهدار باید is an older word order meaning 'you must guard (hold back) the tongue' — نگهدار functions as a verb/imperative 'keep/guard' and باید after it expresses necessity ('must'). بدن begins the next clause ('the body' as topic/subject for what follows).
segments.s580-g14: نباید is the impersonal negative modal 'one must not'; روان را is the direct object 'the soul'; به marks the relation 'to/with', here working as 'to' or 'with'; زهر آژدن is a compound noun 'dragon-poison' (زهر = poison, آژدن = dragon). The clause literally says 'one must not [expose/subject] the soul to dragon-poison.'
segments.s580-g15: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; بر انجمن = 'in/at the assembly' (بر + انجمن), مرد is the person referenced ('man'), بسیار qualifies the speech as 'much/very' and گوی here functions as 'sayer/speaks' (poetic/condensed verb/noun form meaning 'one who says/speaks much'). So: 'who in the assembly speaks much.'
segments.s580-g16: بکاهد is the present/poetic 3rd-person form of کاستن with the prefix ب-: 'he lessens' or 'he reduces'; به here means 'by' (the means), گفتار خود = 'his own speech/words' (گفتار = speech, خود = his own), and آب‌روی = 'reputation/face/honor' is the thing diminished. Word order places the verb first (poetic) then the instrument and the object.
segments.s580-g41: جهاندارمان is جهاندار + the possessive suffix -مان ‘our’, so ‘our sovereign’. باد here is the classical optative particle (’may it be’) following a nominal wish. فریادرس is a predicate noun meaning ‘rescuer/succour’ and completes the optative construction (“may our sovereign be a rescuer”).
segments.s580-g42: که introduces a subordinate/result clause after the optative (’that/so that’). تخت بزرگی = ‘a great throne’ (تخت ‘throne’ + بزرگی ‘greatness/large’ used adjectivally). نماند is the negative form of ماندن here with a jussive/subjunctive force tied to the preceding wish (’not be left’). به کس = ‘to/for anyone’ (کس indefinite).
segments.s580-g43: بگفت is the simple past narrative ‘he said’. این is the pronoun ‘this’ referring to the preceding words. و links the clauses. از پیش برخاستند = ‘they rose/rose up from before (him/in his presence)’: از پیش sets the source/place and برخاستند is the past plural ‘they rose/they departed’.
segments.s580-g44: ز یزدان = ‘from God’ (ز poetic for از). برو آفرین is a frozen/idiomatic phrase here: آفرین ‘acclamation/praise/blessing’ and برو is literally ‘go’ but in this context functions idiomatically as part of the intensive phrase meaning a strong/fervent acclamation or blessing. خواستند is the past plural ‘they asked/sought’.
segments.s580-g45: چو شد is a temporal conjunction ‘when it became/when it came to pass’. سالیان پنج بر چار ماه is a classical numerative phrase: سالیان ‘years’ with عدد پنج ‘five’, and بر چار ماه links the months (‘and four months’) — together ‘five years and four months’. The verb شد carries the sense of ‘had elapsed/it became so’.
segments.s580-g46: بشد here is a narrative/formal past-like particle meaning ‘it happened/there came about’ and introduces the following event. شاه is the subject ‘the king’. روزی is a temporal adverb ‘one day’ (classical usage). به نخچیرگاه = ‘to the hunting-ground’ with به as preposition.
segments.s580-g47: جهان شد پر از is the common pattern ‘the world became full of…’ (شد + adjective/phrase پر از = ‘became full of’). یوز ‘cheetahs’ and سگ ‘dogs/hounds’ are literal; باران here is used figuratively as ‘a shower/flood’ (a mass/host) — i.e. many of them. The coordinating و links the list of creatures.
segments.s580-g48: چه is an exclamatory ‘what/how’ (introducing astonishment). پرنده ‘birds’ names one group. چند ‘how many/several’ asks about number. تازان is the plural of تاز meaning ‘raiders/fast ones’ (here hunting coursers or riders). به تگ = ‘in single file’ or ‘in a narrow line’: به (preposition) + تگ (single/narrow) forming an adverbial phrase describing formation.
segments.s580-g57: فروبرده (فرو برده) is a perfective compound meaning 'has thrust down / plunged' and here functions as the first action. چوب ستاره is a noun phrase (چوب 'stick/club' + ستاره 'star') forming the object—an epithet-like compound for a club (literally 'star-club' or 'club of the star'). بکند is the older, poetic 3rd-person form of کرد/کردن ('to do, to make') used here in coordination with the following verb to present successive actions; in this context it links the thrusting action to the subsequent strike.
segments.s580-g58: بزد is the simple past 3rd-person of زدن 'to strike, to hit'. بر is the preposition 'on/upon', سر is the physical 'head' (direct object of the strike), and شهریار بلند is the noun plus adjective 'the lofty/tall king' (adjective follows the noun in Persian). The phrase literally: 'he struck upon the head [of] the lofty king.'
segments.s580-g59: This phrase places epithets before and after the name: جهانجوی 'world-seeking' (an epithet) + شاپور (name) + جنگی 'warlike/warrior' (appositive). بمرد is an archaic verbal form used here for the event 'died' (in epic style; grammatically related to the root مردن). The overall order is epithet–name–appositive–verb: 'the world-seeking Shapur, a warrior, died.'
segments.s580-g60: کلاه کیی is a compound meaning 'the Kayanian crown' (کلاه 'hat/crown' modified by کیی 'Kayanian'). دیگری را marks the recipient/object with را (here 'another' as the one who receives); سپرد is the past of سپردن 'to entrust/hand over'. Word order is object ('کلاه کیی') then indirect/recipient ('دیگری را') then verb ('سپرد').
segments.s580-g61: This is a catalog of four short verbs (imperative/jussive sense) joined by و: میاز, مناز, متاز, مرنج — each a concise admonition in poetic diction (roughly: 'do not covet / do not dispute / do not boast / do not be offended'). The verbs omit pronouns (understood 'you'); the repeated و simply strings the prohibitions together.
segments.s580-g62: چه ... و چه ... here functions as 'whether ... or ...'. تازی and نازی are nouns describing two types of people (تازی = 'Arab'; نازی = 'a coquettish/proud one'). به کین and به گنج are prepositional phrases giving motive ('for/with vengeance' and 'for/with treasure'). The clause thus says: 'whether an Arab for vengeance, or a proud one for treasure.'
segments.s580-g63: که introduces an explanatory or content clause ('that' / 'for'), بهر تو اینست = literally 'for you this is' (بهر = 'for', تو = 'you', اینست = 'this is'). زین تیره‌گوی is an archaic/deictic + epithet phrase 'from this dark‑speaking (gloomy) one' (زین here is a classical deictic form attached to the noun; تیره‌گوی is a compound adjective meaning 'dark‑speaking' or 'grim‑talking'). The clause as a whole sets up the reported advice.
segments.s580-g64: هنر جوی is a compound meaning 'a seeker of art/skill' (هنر 'skill/art' + جوی as 'seeker' or 'one who seeks'). و connects the two directives. راز جهان را مجوی is an object phrase 'the secret/mystery of the world' (راز جهان) with را marking it as definite, followed by مجوی, the negative present/jussive form 'do not seek' (imperative prohibition). So: 'seek skill, and do not seek the world's secret.'

sentences.s580-s1: When Shapur took his seat, he was very joyful for Iran and stern in purpose.
sentences.s580-s2: Thus he said: 'O famed one, (to) the wise, the worldly‑wise, and the counsel‑speaking mobeds.'
sentences.s580-s3: Know that whoever utters a lie shall henceforth receive no radiance from us.
sentences.s580-s4: Let no falsehood be among us in counsel, for greatness comes from counsel and is in its proper place.
sentences.s580-s13: If a lowly fellow becomes a man, modest and generous, in freedom with one heart and one disposition.
sentences.s580-s14: He who chooses the middle course in his deeds, God's forgiveness will be sufficient for him.
sentences.s580-s15: The fourth (type), who scatters (things) needlessly and for show, ever seeks fame through boasting.
sentences.s580-s16: A generous man's heart attains both worlds; a base man's heart is not happy for a single day.
sentences.s580-s25: Stars appeared from beside the sleeping‑quarters; when something happened the king was unsettled.
sentences.s580-s26: He drank three cups of kingly wine, grew pensive, and turned his head toward sleep.
sentences.s580-s27: The whole army was scattered when the king's host fell asleep.
sentences.s580-s28: He slept, and a wind rose from the plain so that no one would have such a thing in memory.
sentences.s580-s33: If, by a stratagem, you find it useful, do not investigate — do not go circling about its secret.
sentences.s580-s34: Such is the action of this wheel of fate — whether with the young man or with the old man.
sentences.s580-s9: If a man of learning speaks, you should listen so that knowledge will not grow old.
sentences.s580-s10: The heart of the covetous man was full of sorrow; do not turn toward greed while you are able.
sentences.s580-s11: Do not take friendship with one who tests by falsehood; likewise, do not (befriend) a man of corrupt mind.
sentences.s580-s12: The body's constitution was of four essences; passage from these four was rare.
sentences.s580-s17: In this world he had an ugly reputation; in that world he will not attain Paradise.
sentences.s580-s18: A boasting man will not attain the two worlds, for he scatters his desires on vain things.
sentences.s580-s19: He is praiseworthy who chooses the middle course; he praised his own person.
sentences.s580-s20: May the Creator of the world be your companion; may the head of fortune always be alert.
sentences.s580-s5: You will not find that same low-born man to be a lover of mine in the inner garden like the beloved.
sentences.s580-s6: Where there is little brain in a head, it will not suffice; one should not give testimony against anyone.
sentences.s580-s7: Guard your tongue; the body must not expose the soul to dragon-poison.
sentences.s580-s8: He who speaks much in an assembly lessens his reputation by his own words.
sentences.s580-s21: May our sovereign be a rescuer, so that no great throne remain for anyone.
sentences.s580-s22: He said this and they rose up; from God they asked for an acclamation/blessing.
sentences.s580-s23: When five years and four months had passed, one day the king went to the hunting-ground.
sentences.s580-s24: The world became full of cheetahs, a flood-like host, and hounds; what birds and how many raiders in single file!
sentences.s580-s29: He thrust down the star-club and struck the lofty king upon the head.
sentences.s580-s30: The world-seeking Shapur, a warrior, died; he handed the Kayanian crown to another.
sentences.s580-s31: Do not covet, do not contend, do not boast, do not be offended; whether an Arab seeks revenge or a proud one seeks treasure.
sentences.s580-s32: For you this is (the message) from this dark‑tongued one: seek skill, and do not seek the world's secret.
